heading: Matching funds requirement for research and extension activities at eligible institutions
label: 3222d Matching funds requirement for research and extension activities at eligible institutions
notes: References in TextAct of , referred to in subsec. (a)(1), is , , popularly known as the Agricultural College Act of 1890 and also as the Second Morrill Act, which is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 321 et seq.) of chapter 13 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.August 30, 1890act Aug. 30, 1890, ch. 84126 Stat. 417section 321 of this title
Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Amendments2008—Subsec. (c). , substituted “the State shall provide equal matching funds” for “for each of fiscal years 2003 through 2007, the State shall provide matching funds” and struck out at end “Such matching funds shall be for an amount equal to not less than—Pub. L. 110–246, § 7127“(1) 60 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution for fiscal year 2003;“(2) 70 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution for fiscal year 2004;“(3) 80 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution for fiscal year 2005;“(4) 90 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution for fiscal year 2006; and“(5) 100 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution for fiscal year 2007 and each fiscal year thereafter.”2002—Subsec. (c). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the distribution of formula funds to an eligible institution shall be subject to the following matching requirements:Pub. L. 107–171, § 7212(1)“(1) For fiscal year 2000, the State shall provide matching funds from non-Federal sources in an amount equal to not less than 30 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution.“(2) For fiscal year 2001, the State shall provide matching funds from non-Federal sources in an amount equal to not less than 45 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution.“(3) For fiscal year 2002 and each fiscal year thereafter, the State shall provide matching funds from non-Federal sources in an amount equal to not less than 50 percent of the formula funds to be distributed to the eligible institution.”Subsec. (d). , amended heading and text of subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 107–171, § 7212(2)“(1) .—Notwithstanding subsection (f) of this section, the Secretary may waive the matching funds requirement under subsection (c)(1) of this section for fiscal year 2000 for an eligible institution of a State if the Secretary determines that, based on the report received under subsection (b) of this section, the State will be unlikely to satisfy the matching requirement.Fiscal year 2000“(2) .—The Secretary may not waive the matching requirement under subsection (c) of this section for any fiscal year other than fiscal year 2000.”Future fiscal years

children.heading: Eligible institution
children.label: 1 Eligible institution
children.text: The term “eligible institution” means a college eligible to receive funds under the Act of ( et seq.) (commonly known as the “Second Morrill Act”), including Tuskegee University.August 30, 18907 U.S.C. 321

children.heading: Formula funds
children.label: 2 Formula funds
children.text: The term “formula funds” means the formula allocation funds distributed to eligible institutions under sections 3221 and 3222 of this title.

children.heading: Determination of non-Federal sources of funds
children.label: b Determination of non-Federal sources of funds

children.text: the sources of non-Federal funds made available by the State to the eligible institution for agricultural research, extension, and education to meet the requirements of this section; and
the amount of such funds generally available from each source.

children.heading: Matching formula
children.label: c Matching formula
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the State shall provide equal matching funds from non-Federal sources.

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: d Waiver authority
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (f), the Secretary may waive the matching funds requirement under subsection (c) above the 50 percent level for any fiscal year for an eligible institution of a State if the Secretary determines that the State will be unlikely to satisfy the matching requirement.

children.heading: Use of matching funds
children.label: e Use of matching funds
children.text: Under terms and conditions established by the Secretary, matching funds provided as required by subsection (c) may be used by an eligible institution for agricultural research, extension, and education activities.

children.heading: Redistribution required
children.label: 1 Redistribution required
children.text: Federal funds that are not matched by a State in accordance with subsection (c) for a fiscal year shall be redistributed by the Secretary to eligible institutions whose States have satisfied the matching funds requirement for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 2 Administration
children.text: Any redistribution of funds under this subsection shall be subject to the applicable matching requirement specified in subsection (c) and shall be made in a manner consistent with sections 3221 and 3222 of this title, as determined by the Secretary.
